text: The surgeon performs a laparoscopic repair on the cystic duct using a 4-0 PD suture, carefully closing the duct over a 12 French T-tube. The repair is visible in the small anatomical space of the patient.

The surgeon uses a laparoscopic knot pusher to re-approximate the cystic duct, successfully bringing the duct together over the repair. After placing the first suture, an additional suture is inserted to secure the repair.
The surgeon demonstrates the small size of the patient's common duct, showing the placement of a T-tube and the use of two sutures to approximate the edges of the duct.

The surgeon performs a cholangiogram through the T-tube at the end of the procedure, visually confirming good bile flow into the duodenum. The T-tube is planned for removal in four weeks post-repair to allow the common duct to scar down.
